page: Microsoft Azure F4-1amds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Famdsv7 server family. Powered by an AMD processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 1 dedicated vCPU and 32.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per core, classifying the server as a memory-intensive resource.

The server comes equipped with 472 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk configurations. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. There are no hardware GPU accelerators provisioned on this instance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F4-1amds_v7 offers a high density of memory relative to its single-core compute allocation, making it a specialized option for workloads that require substantial RAM without needing multi-threaded CPU scaling. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, lightweight database hosting, and enterprise workloads requiring dedicated single-core execution with a large memory footprint.
description: Microsoft Azure F4-1amds_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Famdsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a single dedicated AMD vCPU paired with 32.0 GB of system memory, delivering a high memory-to-core ratio of 32 GB per vCPU. The instance includes 472 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, though it does not bundle any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU hardware. This memory-intensive configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity without the overhead of multi-core CPU licensing or allocation. It is suited for memory-optimized tasks, lightweight database hosting, caching layers, and enterprise applications that demand dedicated single-core performance alongside a large memory footprint.
